flashcards.back: The fundamental process by which a CPU retrieves instructions from memory, interprets them, and carries out the required operations.
A signal sent to the CPU that indicates an event needing immediate attention, causing the CPU to suspend current activities and execute an interrupt service routine.
A feature that allows certain hardware subsystems to access main memory independently of the CPU, improving data transfer efficiency for I/O operations.

flashcards.front: What are the components of the CPU?
flashcards.back: Control Unit, Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU), and Registers (including Program Counter, Instruction Register, and others).
The arrangement of different types of memory in a computer system based on speed, cost, and capacity, typically including registers, cache, main memory, and secondary storage.
